perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, Agency serves agent to one or more suppliers/ manufacturers (“ Principals ”) in providing agency sales and marketing services in the foodservice trade channel (“Agency Sales and Marketing Services ”), and WHEREAS, in the course of the provision of Agency Sales and Marketing Services for Agency ’s Princi pals, each party (a “Disclosing Party” ) may disclose to the other party (the “Recipient” ) certain Confidential Information (as such term is defined herein below), which shall be deemed to be confidential; and WHEREAS, the Confidential Information may be disclosed in many such form(s) including oral and/or written disclosure, disclosure through training and/or disclosure through observation of products or prototypes of products, services, business plans, business documents, materials and/or property;
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, Agency serves agent to one or more suppliers/ manufacturers (“ Principals ”) in providing agency sales and marketing services in the foodservice trade channel (“Agency Sales and Marketing Services ”), and WHEREAS, in the course of the provision of Agency Sales and Marketing Services for Agency ’s Princi pals, each party (a “Providing Party” ) may disclose to the other party (the “Recipient” ) certain Confidential Information (as such term is defined herein below), which shall be deemed to be confidential; and WHEREAS, the Confidential Information may be disclosed in many such form(s) including oral and/or written disclosure, disclosure through training and/or disclosure through observation of products or prototypes of products, services, business plans, business documents, materials and/or property;
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Disclosing Party' to 'Providing Party' introduces ambiguity. Throughout the document, 'Disclosing Party' is used, and suddenly we introduce another term to refer to the same concept, which is not defined nor is it clear in the context. This creates contradiction, as it is unclear which party to which the 'Disclosing Party' refers. The change introduces confusion and therefore it becomes uncertain which party is subject to the obligations. The definition of the obligation becomes subject to interpretation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Documents made available hereunder, including all copies, notes, summaries, and abstracts thereof, shall be returned to the Disclosing Party or destroyed upon completion of the Agen cy Sales and Marketing Services with Distributor or upon written request by the Disclosing Party, and Recipient shall certify in writing that it has complied with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Disclosing Party' to 'Providing Party' introduces a new, undefined term within the contract, while the 'Contradicted Text' refers specifically to 'Disclosing Party'. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because it becomes unclear to whom the obligation to return or destroy documents applies. The term 'Disclosing Party' is used elsewhere, and this new term creates ambiguity and makes the contradicted text uncertain, thus satisfying the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall (i) maintain the Confidential Information i n strict confidence and take all reasonable steps to prevent its disclosure to third parties; (ii) use at least the same degree of care as Recipient uses in maintaining the secrecy of its own Confidential Information (but no less than a reasonable degree o f care); and (iii) prevent the removal of any proprietary, confidential or copyright notices placed on the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall (i) maintain the Confidential Information in reasonable confidence and take commercially reasonable steps to prevent its disclosure to third parties; (ii) use at least the same degree of care as Recipient uses in maintaining the secrecy of its own Confidential Information (but no less than a minimal degree of care); and (iii) prevent the removal of any proprietary, confidential or copyright notices placed on the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'strict confidence' to 'reasonable confidence' and 'reasonable degree of care' to 'minimal degree of care' introduces ambiguity and lowers the standard of care. While the original requires a high standard, the changed text introduces a subjective and lower standard. This creates a contradiction because it is unclear which standard the Recipient is actually held to, creating uncertainty in the enforcement of this provision.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is understood and agreed that damages are an inadequate remedy in the event of a breach or intended or threatened breach by any Party under this Agreement and that any such breach by any party will cause irreparable harm, injury and damage; accordingly, the Parties agree that the damaged party may be entitled, without waiving any additional rights or remedies (including monetary damages) otherwise available to it at law, or in equity, or by statute, to seek preliminary and permanent injunctive relief in the event of a breach or intended or threatened breach by any Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text lowers the standard of care required of the Recipient regarding Confidential Information from 'strict confidence' and 'reasonable degree of care' to 'reasonable confidence' and 'minimal degree of care'. This contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' because the original agreement stipulated damages were an inadequate remedy, implying a high degree of protection was expected for Confidential Information. By lowering this standard, the changed text weakens the basis for the irreparable harm claim, creating an inconsistency regarding the level of protection afforded to the Confidential Information and thus a contradiction impacting potential enforcement of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: (b) Recipient may use the Confidential Information only in connection with the performance of the Agency Sales and Ma rketing Services with Distributor . Recipient shall not, at any time, make any use of the Confidential Information for any other purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) Recipient may utilize the Confidential Data only in connection with the performance of the Agency Sales and Ma rketing Services with Distributor . Recipient shall not, at any time, make any use of the Confidential Information for any other purpose.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Confidential Data' in the first sentence creates a contradiction. 'Confidential Information' is a defined term, and the introduction of 'Confidential Data' without definition creates confusion about the scope of permitted use. While the second sentence uses the original defined term, the first sentence implies a different, undefined scope. Which then it is unclear what is the Recipient allows to use, is it only the Confidetial Information as it is defined, or the Confidential Data in the term of the sales and marketing services.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definition . “Confidential Information” means, in respect to each Party: (a) any information in which such Party claims a proprietary and/or confidential interest; (b) all confidential matters of such Party including, without limitation, technical kn ow-how, trade secrets, technical data, new product strategy, new products or processes, analysis, compilations, concepts, technical processes, formulae, recipes, specifications, inventions, research projects, customer lists, pricing, pricing policies, operational methods, financial information, marketing information and other business affairs; (c) any information of a confidential nature concerning such Party’s customers, suppliers or employees; and (d) any information such Party has received from othe rs which they are obliged to treat as proprietary and/or confidential . The Confidential Information of either Party includes all of the above information with respect to any entity controlling, controlled by, or under common control with, such Party. Confidential Information shall not include information which: (a) is in the public domain prior to the date of its disclosure to Recipient by the Disclosing Party; (b) is known and can be shown to be known by Recipient prior to the date of its disclos ure to Recipient by Disclosing Party; (c) becomes part of the public domain by publication or otherwise, and is not the result of any unauthorized act or omission on the part of Recipient; (d) can be demonstrated to have been supplied to Recipient by a thi rd party who is under no obligation to the Disclosing Party to maintain such information in confidence; or (e) is independently developed by Recipient without the use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Confidential Data' in the first sentence of the paragraph. 'Confidential Information' is a defined term in the contract as described in the Contradicted Text. Introducing the term 'Confidential Data' without a definition creates ambiguity and a contradiction, as it's unclear whether 'Confidential Data' falls under the defined 'Confidential Information' or represents a different scope of data. The second sentence still uses 'Confidential Information', further fueling the confusion. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition because the contract uses multiple terms without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations and uncertainty about which data is restricted, resulting in a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All Documents made available hereunder, including all copies, notes, summaries, and abstracts thereof, shall be returned to the Disclosing Party or destroyed upon completion of the Agen cy Sales and Marketing Services with Distributor or upon written request by the Disclosing Party, and Recipient shall certify in writing that it has complied with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: All Records made available hereunder, including all copies, notes, summaries, and abstracts thereof, shall be returned to the Providing Party or destroyed upon completion of the Agen cy Sales and Marketing Services with Distributor or upon written request by the Providing Party, and Recipient shall certify in writing that it has complied with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Documents' to 'Records' creates ambiguity and potential contradiction. 'Documents' is the original term, and by changing it to 'Records', without a definition, the scope of what needs to be returned or destroyed is now unclear. Does this 'Records' include only the Documents provided, or it also includes other material? As the new term is introduced, is unclear which party to which the 'Providing Party' refers.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall keep the Confidential Information confidential at all times and shall not disclose the Confid ential Information to any person including its employees except to its employees, representatives, advisors and agents who have a need to know such information in connection with assisting Recipient for the purposes set forth in paragraph 2(b) above and wh o are required to keep such information confidential.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Documents' to 'Records' introduces a misaligned terminology issue, as explained in the perturbation explanation. The original text clearly defined the scope as 'Documents'. The changed text uses 'Records' without definition, creating ambiguity and potentially broadening the scope of what must be returned or destroyed. The contradicted text refers to 'Confidential Information', which may or may not be included within the new term 'Records'. The ambiguity creates uncertainty and therefore constitutes an in-text contradiction, as the obligations regarding Confidential Information are now unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Patent, Copyright or Trademark Infringement . Nothing in this Agre ement is intended to grant any new or additional rights under any patent, copyright or trademark, nor shall this Agreement grant the Parties any new or additional rights in or to the Confidential Information, except the limited right to review and use such Confidential Information for the purpose outlined herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Patent, Copyright or Trademark Violation. Nothing in this Agre ement is intended to grant any new or additional rights under any patent, copyright or trademark, nor shall this Agreement grant the Parties any new or additional rights in or to the Confidential Information, except the limited right to review and use such Confidential Information for the purpose outlined herein.
perturbation.explanation: The heading change from 'Infringement' to 'Violation' introduces ambiguity. While generally understood, 'infringement' is the specific legal term. Using 'violation' alongside 'patent, copyright or trademark' (which usually pair with 'infringement') creates a contradiction as to what standard applies. Is a 'violation' the same as an 'infringement' in terms of legal recourse? This change creates confusion of the legal standard to be enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is understood and agreed that damages are an inadequate remedy in the event of a breach or intended or threatened breach by any Party under this Agreement and that any such breach by any party will cause irreparable harm, injury and damage; accordingly, the Parties agree that the damaged party may be entitled, without waiving any additional rights or remedies (including monetary damages) otherwise available to it at law, or in equity, or by statute, to seek preliminary and permanent injunctive relief in the event of a breach or intended or threatened breach by any Party.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Infringement' to 'Violation' introduces ambiguity regarding the legal standard. The contradicted text refers to 'breach' and 'irreparable harm'. If 'violation' is interpreted as a less severe action than 'infringement' (which is a legally defined term), it creates a contradiction as to whether the threshold for 'breach' and 'irreparable harm' is met. The original text using 'infringement' provided a clearer and more legally sound basis for triggering the remedies outlined in the contradicted text. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', making enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Governing Law . THIS AGREEMENT SHALL BE GOVERNE D BY THE LAWS OF THE STATE OF THE PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS OF DISTRIBUTOR AS SET FORTH IN THE FIRST PARAGRAPH ABOVE (REGARDLESS OF THE LAWS THAT MIGHT OTHERWISE GOVERN UNDER APPLICABLE PRINCIPLES OF CONFLICTS OF LAW) AS TO ALL MATTERS, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO MATTERS OF VALIDITY, CONSTRUCTION, EFFECT, PERFORMANCE AND REMEDY.
perturbation.changed_text: Applicable Law. THIS AGREEMENT SHALL BE GOVERNE D BY THE STATUTES OF THE STATE OF THE PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS OF DISTRIBUTOR AS SET FORTH IN THE FIRST PARAGRAPH ABOVE (REGARDLESS OF THE LAWS THAT MIGHT OTHERWISE GOVERN UNDER APPLICABLE PRINCIPLES OF CONFLICTS OF LAW) AS TO ALL MATTERS, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO MATTERS OF VALIDITY, CONSTRUCTION, EFFECT, PERFORMANCE AND REMEDY.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Governing Law' to 'Applicable Law' but then using 'statutes' instead of 'laws' creates a contradiction. While 'laws' is a broad term, 'statutes' specifically refers to legislation. This limits the scope of the governing legal framework, creating confusion. If the contract is governed by 'statutes', does that exclude common law principles or other regulations? This introduces ambiguity. It's no longer clear what source of law is the contract governed by.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event Re cipient is requested or required by applicable law, regulation or legal process (including a subpoena or other administrative or judicial request), to disclose any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, Recipient shall promptly notify the Disclo sing Party so that it may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy. Unless the demand shall have been timely limited, quashed or extended, Recipient shall thereafter be entitled to comply with such demand to the extent required by law. If requested by the Disclosing Party, Recipient shall provide reasonable cooperation (at the expense of the Disclosing Party) in the defense of a demand to disclose Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Governing Law' with 'Applicable Law' and uses 'statutes' instead of 'laws'. This creates a contradiction because 'statutes' is a narrower term than 'laws'. The contradicted text refers to 'applicable law, regulation or legal process'. By limiting the 'Applicable Law' to 'statutes', it introduces ambiguity as to whether 'regulation or legal process' is still covered, thus creating a contradiction.
